---
title: "MCP & Connecting AI to Data & Tools"
---

::: {.topic-badges}
[Advanced]{.level-badge .level-advanced} · **Technical & Agentic** track · ~1 hour, self-paced
:::

[← Back to the Technical & Agentic track](technical-agentic.qmd){.track-back}

*Understand how assistants connect to real systems — and the access risks that come with it.*

## What you'll be able to do

- Explain what MCP (Model Context Protocol) does
- Recognize when connecting AI to a tool helps vs. adds risk
- Apply least-privilege and approval before connecting to data

## Overview

The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard way to connect an assistant to outside data sources and tools — a file store, a database, a calendar. It's the plumbing behind agents that do more than chat. Powerful, and a new surface for mistakes and access risk.

Connect with least privilege: give the narrowest access that does the job, prefer read-only, and confirm Drexel's rules before connecting to any real or sensitive system.

## Practice activities

```{=html}
<details class="activity"><summary>Activity 1 · Advanced — Map a connection</summary>
```

1. Pick a task that would need AI to reach a tool or data source.
2. Write what access it truly needs — and the **narrowest** version that works.
3. Decide read-only vs. write, and what stays off-limits.

```{=html}
</details>
```

```{=html}
<details class="activity"><summary>Activity 2 · Advanced — Risk-check it</summary>
```

1. List what could go wrong if the connection misbehaves.
2. Add a human-approval step before any write or send action.

```{=html}
</details>
```

## Check your readiness

Answer these, then check — your score suggests whether to dive in or skim the readings first.

<div class="readiness-quiz">
<script type="application/json" class="quiz-data">
{"questions": [{"q": "MCP is, in plain terms…", "options": ["A standard way to connect AI to data and tools", "A chatbot", "A statistic", "A file format"], "answer": 0, "explain": "It's the connection layer for agents."}, {"q": "The right access posture is…", "options": ["Least privilege — narrowest access, prefer read-only", "Full admin", "Whatever's easiest", "No limits"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Minimize the blast radius."}, {"q": "Before connecting AI to a real system you should…", "options": ["Confirm Drexel's data rules and approvals", "Just connect it", "Disable logging", "Share credentials"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Approval and data classification come first."}]}
</script>
</div>

## Recommended readings

From the AI readings library (ask the facilitator for access):

- **How Agents Use Systems Differently (Davis Treybig)** — what changes when AI connects to systems.
- **A Practical Guide to Building Agents (OpenAI)** — tools and connections in agent design.

## Useful resources

- [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io) — the open MCP standard.
- [ChatGPT Edu (Drexel AI Tools)](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/tools) — the approved tool, with data rules.

::: {.callout-warning}
## Data rules
Connect with **least privilege**; get approval before any connection to real or sensitive data. See [Tools & Access](../tools.qmd) and Drexel's [AI Tools page](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/tools).
:::
